title: Subarray Equality Validation with Consecutive Elements Swap

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers `nums` of length `n`, determine if there exists a subarray that can be obtained by swapping the consecutive elements of a subarray.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided in a single line, `n`, followed by `n` space-separated integers.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a boolean value indicating whether a valid subarray exists.
statement.notes: Assume that all elements in the array are distinct and that the array contains at least one element.

examples.explanation: Subarray [2,3] can be obtained by swapping consecutive elements.

hints.text: Use two pointers technique to track subarray equality.

editorial.title: Subarray Equality Validation
editorial.intuition: Maintain two pointers, one at the start of the subarray and one at the end.
editorial.bruteForce: Compare all possible subarrays and check if they can be obtained by swapping consecutive elements.
editorial.betterSolution: Use two pointers to track subarray equality.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof based on the two-pointer technique.
editorial.dryRun: Example run using the two-pointer technique.
